Q1 Not checked

Consider the following statements about the Global Innovation Index (GII):
1. It is released annually by the World Economic Forum.
2. It assesses economies based on investment, technology, adoption, and socio-economic impact.
Which of the above statements is/are correct?

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
• GII is released by the World Intellectual Property Organisation (WIPO), not the WEF.
• It measures innovation performance across investment, technology, and socio-economic
indicators and is UN-recognized for STI policy reference.

2. The “special de-merit rate” replaces the earlier compensation cess on sin/luxury goods.
Which of the above are correct?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
• New Rate Structure with 2 slabs 5% and 18% replacing 12% and 28%.
• 40% Special De-Merit Rate on sin/luxury goods replacing compensation cess.

Q5 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

With reference to the recent Sample Registration System (SRS) Statistical Report 2023 consider the following statements:
1. The Total Fertility Rate (TFR) for India in 2023 stands below the replacement level of 2.1.
2. Bihar continues to have the highest Crude Birth Rate (CBR) among Indian states.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
• India’s TFR is 1.9, below the replacement rate of 2.1. The rural TFR (2.1) is higher than urban TFR (1.5).
• Bihar records the highest CBR (25.8) in 2023, while Tamil Nadu and Delhi have the lowest.

1. It is the second ship in the Arnala-class series of ASW SWCs.
2. It is powered by a diesel engine-waterjet combination.
3. It is equipped with lightweight torpedoes and indigenous ASW rockets.
Which of the statements given above are correct?

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
• INS Androth is the second ship after Arnala in the ASW Shallow Water Craft (SWC) series.
• The ship uses diesel engine-waterjet propulsion, equipped with lightweight torpedoes and
indigenous ASW rockets for coastal submarine warfare.

Q14 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following statements regarding criteria for Biodiversity Hotspot:
1. A Biodiversity Hotspot must contain at least 15,000 species of vascular plants as endemics.
2. Biodiversity Hotspot has to have lost at least 70% of its original habitat.
Which of the above statements is/are correct?

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
• A Biodiversity Hotspot must contain at least 1500 species of vascular plants as endemics.
• A biodiversity hotspot is defined as a region with a high concentration of endemic species
and a significant level of habitat loss. While the presence of a large number of endemic vascular
plant species is considered a key criterion, the more critical factor is the degree of habitat loss.
• Having lost at least 70% of its original vegetation indicates a high level of threat to the unique
biodiversity within that region.

Q15 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Which of the following protocols originated from Rio Earth Summit, 1992?
1. The United Nations Framework Convention on Climate Change (UNFCCC)
2. The Convention on Biological Diversity (CBD)
3. Aichi Targets
4. Kyoto Protocol
Select the correct answer using the codes given below:

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
• The Aichi Targets are a set of 20 biodiversity targets adopted by the Conference of the
Parties (COP) to the Convention on Biological Diversity (CBD) in 2010.
• The Kyoto Protocol is an international agreement that was adopted in 1997 under the UNFCCC.
While it is related to climate change, it did not originate at the Rio Earth Summit.

Consider the following statements about National Green Tribunal (NGT):
1. The National Green Tribunal has jurisdiction over all civil cases relating to the environment.
2. The NGT is mandated to make and endeavour for disposal of applications or appeals finally within 6 months of filing of the same.
3. The decision of the NGT is final and cannot be challenged in any Court.
Which of the above statements is/are correct?

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
An appeal against order/decision/ award of the NGT lies to the Supreme Court, generally within
90 days from the date of communication.

Q20 Not checked

Consider the following statements:
1. Currently, India has a total of 75 Ramsar Wetland sites.
2. ‘Montreux Record’ registers all the Ramsar Wetland sites all over the world.
Which of the above statements is/are correct?

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
• As of September 2025, India has a total of 91 Ramsar Wetland sites.
• Montreux Record is a register of wetland sites on the List of Ramsar wetlands of international
importance where changes in ecological character have occurred, are occurring, or are likely
to occur as a result of technological developments, pollution or other human interference.

Q23 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
1. Nagoya Protocol is concerned with governing the movements of Living Modified Organisms (LMOs).
2. Cartagena and Nagoya Protocols are laid down under Convention on Biological Diversity (CBD).
Which of the above statements is/are INCORRECT?

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
• The Nagoya Protocol on Access to Genetic Resources and the Fair and Equitable Sharing of Benefits Arising from their Utilization to the Convention on Biological Diversity is an international agreement which aims at sharing the benefits arising from the utilization of
genetic resources in a fair and equitable way.
• Both the Cartagena Protocol on Biosafety and the Nagoya Protocol on Access to Genetic
Resources and the Fair and Equitable Sharing of Benefits Arising from their Utilization are
supplementary agreements to the Convention on Biological Diversity (CBD).

2. Oil spills result in hyperthermia which causes fatalities in birds and animals.
3. The Dobson unit measures the total amount of O3 in an overhead column of the atmosphere.
Which of the above statements is/are correct?

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
Oil spills result in hypothermia which causes fatalities in birds and animals.

Q25 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
1. The transportation sector is one of the major contributors of Greenhouse gas emissions.
2. Carbon sequestration is the process of capturing and storing atmospheric carbon dioxide.
3. The most potent Greenhouse gas is water vapor.
Which of the above statements is/are correct?

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
• While water vapor is the most abundant greenhouse gas in the atmosphere, its concentration
is primarily determined by temperature, making it a feedback mechanism rather than the
most potent greenhouse gas in terms of its direct impact on warming.
• More potent greenhouse gases include potent anthropogenic greenhouse gases like carbon
dioxide, methane, and chlorofluorocarbons (CFCs).

Q26 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider following statements:
1. Impact printers use physical contact with paper to produce an image.
2. Dot-matrix printers use small electromagnetically activated pins in the print head and inked ribbon to produce images by impact.
Which of the above statements is/are correct?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
• Impact printers create images by striking a tool against an inked ribbon.
• Dot matrix printers is a type of impact printer that works by using pins to strike an inked
ribbon and transfer ink in the form of dots onto paper to create characters.
Hence, both the statements are correct.

1. NFC is a short-range wireless connectivity technology.
2. It allows NFC-enabled devices to communicate with each other and transfer information quickly and easily with a single touch.
3. Any of the devices must contain NFC chips, as transactions take place within a very short distance.
Which of the above statements is/are correct?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
NFC allows communication between two electronic devices such as phones, smart watches, etc.
placed at very short distances, say up to 10 cm. For NFC to work, both the devices must contain
NFC chips.

Q32 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following statements about Quantum Dot Display:
1. Quantum Dot Display does not need a back light and are self-illuminating structures.
2. They are 3-D confined structures.
Which of the above statements is/are correct?

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
• Quantum dots are excited by the blue light from the display panel to emit pure basic colors,
which reduces light losses and color crosstalk in color filters, improving display brightness
and color gamut. Hence, statement 1 is incorrect.
• Quantum dots (QD) are nanoparticles/structures that exhibit 3-dimensional quantum
confinement, which leads to many unique optical and transport properties. Hence, statement
2 is correct.

Q38 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

What is the 1st stage of Pugh model?

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
Pugh method compares each concept relative to a reference or datum concept. The steps involved
in concept selection method are:
1. Choose the criteria to evaluate concept.
2. Formulate decision matrix.
3. Clarify decision concepts.
4. Choose the datum concept.
5. Complete the matrix entries.
6. Evaluate the ratings.
7. Establish a new datum and return the matrix.
8. Examine the selected concept for improvement.

Q49 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Which of the following can be defined as a resource allocation technique for a project that is to be completed by a specified due-date?

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
Resource levelling is a resource allocation technique used for a project that is to be completed by a specified due-data.
Resource smoothing is a resource allocation technique used in situations where there is no constraint
on project completion time, but there are constraints with regard to availability of other resources.

Q50 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following statements with regard to merits of Lump-sum contracts:
1. As the total cost of the work is known before hand, the owner can arrange funds.
2. Detailed measurements of the work done are not required except when additions and alterations are needed.
3. Due to competition among the contractors, the contractor tries to take the work up even at less profit, resulting in low cost of the work.
Which of the above statements is/are correct?

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
Merits of Lump-sum contract are :
1. As the total cost of the work is known before hand, the owner can arrange the fund in time.
2. Detailed measurements of the work done are not required except for additions and alterations.
3. The contractor’s profit mainly lies in the completion time. Hence for getting more profit the
contractor tries to complete the work as early as possible. This concept on the part of contractor
coincides with the objective of the owner also.
4. As it is difficult to get intermediate payment, the contractor tries to finish earliest to get the
full payment earliest and go to another venture. The owner also benefits from the project
being completed early or in time.
5. Due to competition among the contractors, the contractor tries to take the work up even at
less profit, resulting in low cost of the work.

Q51 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

In the project life cycle, the ability to influence project outcome is:

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
As the project progresses through and past conceptual and planning phase, decisions are already made, resources are committed and contracts are in place. Therefore, the ability to influence decreases sharply.

Q52 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

In project management, which of the following best represents risk exposure?

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
Risk exposure is a function of the probability of occurrence and the consequences.
Risk exposure = Probability of occurrence of risk × Financial impact on occurrence of the risk event

Q57 Not checked

Which of the following statements regarding work breakdown structure are correct?
1. It is a graphical representation.
2. It is incremental and hierarchical decomposition of project phases.
3. It is top-down approach.
4. It is not suitable for big projects under complex conditions.
Which of the above statements are correct?

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
• Work breakdown structure (WBS) is pictorial representation (not graphical).
• It is suitable for big projects under complex conditions.
• The major project is first identified in terms of its end items, then split into system, sub systems,
then their components and elements.

where, a is side of hexagon.

Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Which one of the following polymer is used in the manufacturing of paints and lacquers?

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
Glyptal is a polymer that is synthesized from the monomers - ethylene glycol and phthalic acid. It
forms hard, adherent, and durable films upon drying. These properties make it excellent for use
as a binder in paints and lacquers, providing a tough, glossy finish that resists weathering. The
applications of the given polymers are as below:
Glyptal: In manufacturing of paints and lacquers.
Polypropene: In manufacturing of ropes and toys.
Polyvinyl chloride: In manufacturing of raincoats and handbags.
Bakelite: Making combs and electrical switch.

Q65 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Consider the following statements regarding characteristics of dielectric materials :
1. They have high value of insulation resistance.
2. They do not exhibit polarization phenomena.
3. They can store electrostatic energy.
Which of the above given statements are correct?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
Dielectric exhibits polarization through a progressive orientation of permanent or induced dipoles
when subjected to an external electric field. There are several types of polarization mechanisms in
dielectrics, including electronic, ionic, and orientational polarization.

Q69 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Piezoelectric materials,
1. convert thermal energy into electrical energy.
2. are used in sensors.
3. exhibit the inverse effect of electrostriction.
Which of the above statements are correct?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
Piezoelectric materials converts mechanical energy into electrical energy and hence, are used in
sensors. Thus, it exhibit the inverse effect of electrostriction (conversion of electric energy into
mechanical deformation).

Q91 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Each of the next items consists of two statements, one labelled as the ‘Statement (I)’ and the other as ‘Statement (II)’. You are to examine these two statements carefully and select the answers to these items using the codes given below:
Codes:
(a) Both Statement (I) and Statement (II) are individually true and Statement (II) is the correct explanation of Statement (I).
(b) Both Statement (I) and Statement (II) are individually true but Statement (II) is NOT the correct explanation of Statement (I).
(c) Statement (I) is true but Statement (II) is false.
(d) Statement (I) is false but Statement (II) is true.

Statement (I): PSLV can launch satellites only into Low Earth Orbit (LEO).
Statement (II): It has been used in interplanetary missions like the Mars Orbiter Mission.

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
PSLV can launch satellites into Low Earth Orbit (LEO), Geosynchronous, and Geostationary
orbits - not just LEO. PSLV was used in interplanetary missions like the Mars Orbiter Mission.

Q92 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Each of the next items consists of two statements, one labelled as the ‘Statement (I)’ and the other as ‘Statement (II)’. You are to examine these two statements carefully and select the answers to these items using the codes given below:
Codes:
(a) Both Statement (I) and Statement (II) are individually true and Statement (II) is the correct explanation of Statement (I).
(b) Both Statement (I) and Statement (II) are individually true but Statement (II) is NOT the correct explanation of Statement (I).
(c) Statement (I) is true but Statement (II) is false.
(d) Statement (I) is false but Statement (II) is true.

Statement (I): Caste data, beyond Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes, was never collected in the history of India.
Statement (II): The Registrar General and Census Commissioner (RG&CC) is authorized to design the census proforma.

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
• History of caste census Pre independence –Regularly caste data was collected from 1881 to
1941, but 1941 data couldn’t publish (world war).
• Post-independence (1951) - Discontinued caste enumeration except for SC and ST. Since 1961
– states could conduct their own survey of OBCs.

Q96 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Each of the next items consists of two statements, one labelled as the ‘Statement (I)’ and the other as ‘Statement (II)’. You are to examine these two statements carefully and select the answers to these items using the codes given below:
Codes:
(a) Both Statement (I) and Statement (II) are individually true and Statement (II) is the correct explanation of Statement (I).
(b) Both Statement (I) and Statement (II) are individually true but Statement (II) is NOT the correct explanation of Statement (I).
(c) Statement (I) is true but Statement (II) is false.
(d) Statement (I) is false but Statement (II) is true.

Statement (I): Cache memory is faster than Random Access Memory.
Statement (II): Random Access Memory is closer to the processor than cache memory.

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
Cache memory is faster than the system RAM, and is closer to the CPU because it is on the
processor chip. It acts as a temporary storage area that is used to store frequently accessed data
and thus, is more readily available to the processor than the computer’s main memory source i.e.
RAM or ROM. Hence, Statement (II) is false

Q99 Not checked
Technical • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Each of the next items consists of two statements, one labelled as the ‘Statement (I)’ and the other as ‘Statement (II)’. You are to examine these two statements carefully and select the answers to these items using the codes given below:

(a) Both Statement (I) and Statement (II) are individually true and Statement (II) is the correct explanation of Statement (I).
(b) Both Statement (I) and Statement (II) are individually true but Statement (II) is NOT the correct explanation of Statement (I).
(c) Statement (I) is true but Statement (II) is false.
(d) Statement (I) is false but Statement (II) is true.

Statement (I): Operating characteristic curve is the graph between percentage defective and probability of acceptance.
Statement (II): Type A operating characteristic curve gives the probability of acceptance for lots coming from a continuous process or infinite size lot.

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
There are two types of OC curve:
1. Type-A: It gives the probability of acceptance for an individual lot coming from finite production.
2. Type-B: operating characteristic curve gives the probability of acceptance for lots coming from a continuous process or infinite size lot.
